description: Iraq's invasion of Kuwait triggered a supply shock and fears over Middle East oil flows.
Currency and financial crisis across East Asia sharply reduced regional oil demand.
Weak demand combined with OPEC overproduction drove Brent to multi-year lows near $10.
Terrorist attacks in the US created economic uncertainty and demand fears.
War in a major oil-producing region raised supply-disruption concerns.
Speculative demand and a weak US dollar pushed Brent to a record high near $147.
Collapse of Lehman Brothers triggered a global demand crash and steep price fall.
Political upheaval across North Africa and the Middle East disrupted Libyan output.
OPEC's decision to maintain production amid a US shale glut began a prolonged price collapse.
Oversupply and slowing demand pushed Brent below $30 to a cycle low.
OPEC and allies agreed to coordinated output cuts to rebalance the market.

event_name: US withdraws from Iran nuclear deal
description: Reimposition of US sanctions on Iran threatened to remove barrels from the market.
Pandemic demand collapse plus a Saudi-Russia price war caused a historic price crash.
OPEC+ agreed to the largest coordinated supply cut in history to stabilize prices.
Invasion and ensuing sanctions on a major exporter drove prices sharply higher.
